title: Aechmea lingulata
taxonomy: Aechmea lingulata is classified within the plant family Bromeliaceae, which is a group of flowering plants known as bromeliads. This family includes over 3,000 species of plants, with Aechmea lingulata being one of the many species within it. The Bromeliaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, with Aechmea lingulata belonging to the subfamily Tillandsioideae. Aechmea lingulata's classification provides a better understanding of its evolutionary history and characteristics.

intro: Aechmea lingulata is a species of epiphytic bromeliad, a type of flowering plant that grows on other plants for support. The leaves and inflorescences of Aechmea lingulata have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever reduction, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections and other ailments. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Aechmea lingulata is often used to prepare infusions and decoctions, which are used to treat colds, flu, and other respiratory infections, as well as to soothe sore throats and reduce fever.
